title: AgentSpec.model is type-optional but dispatch-required — call-agent throws 'no model specified' with no fallback
description: AgentSpec at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/types.ts line 25 declares `model?: string` (optional). callAgentTool.execute at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/call-agent-tool.ts line 83 rejects dispatch when modelSpec is absent: `if (!modelSpec) { throw new Error('call-agent: agent X has no model specified.'); }`. A spec passing TypeScript validation with no model field fails at runtime. There is no fallback path — call-agent does not defer to an orchestration-agent default, an ExtensionContext binding, or any other source. The optional-type framing suggests model may be supplied elsewhere; the runtime contradicts that framing.

evidence.reference: FB-005 'model field required at dispatch but optional in AgentSpec type'; actual_behavior: dispatch fails 'call-agent: agent X has no model specified.'

Investigation report FB-005 section confirms type at types.ts:25 and runtime reject at call-agent-tool.ts:83 verbatim; verdict 'Code' — either make model required in the AgentSpec type (Option A, breaking change cascading to all existing .agent.yaml specs without model) or add fallback resolution in call-agent (Option B, deferring to orchestration-agent default); explicitly NET-NEW relative to DEC-0001/0002/0003 and FGAP-115
model?: string; — declares model as optional in the canonical TypeScript type
if (!modelSpec) throw new Error 'call-agent: agent X has no model specified.' — runtime reject with no fallback

impact: Agent authors writing minimal specs (per the TypeScript type contract) ship specs that fail at dispatch with a runtime error. The type's optional-marker promises a flexibility the runtime does not honor. Distinct from FGAP-115 (which addresses bare-model-id resolution against session provider) — FGAP-115 assumes model is present; this gap is the absence-of-field case.
proposed_resolution: Code decision required between two options per the investigation-report verdict. Option A: change AgentSpec.model from `model?: string` to `model: string` (required in type) at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/types.ts:25 — breaking change cascading to every existing spec without a model field. Option B: add fallback resolution at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/call-agent-tool.ts:83 — defer to the orchestration-agent's default model when modelSpec is absent — keeps the type optional but honors the semantics. Pick one and document the contract in the AgentSpec schema (FGAP-156 dependency for canonical-shape parity).
